endpoints.name: Larvitar evolutionary line (Larvitar / Pupitar / Tyranitar)
endpoints.design_thesis: Johto pseudo-legendary line; mountain-eater-to-mountain-mover cost-of-power canon; slowest-evolution Gen II line at level 55 to Tyranitar
endpoints.structural_signature: pseudo-legendary cohort precedent (first such node in graph)

endpoints.name: Rowlet evolutionary line (Rowlet / Dartrix / Decidueye)
endpoints.design_thesis: Alola Grass starter; owl-to-archer feather-blade-to-arrow-quill canon
endpoints.structural_signature: only starter line with secondary-typing-shift at apex (Grass/Flying base+middle, Grass/Ghost or Grass/Fighting apex)

endpoints.name: Pancham evolutionary line (Pancham / Pangoro)
endpoints.design_thesis: Kalos bancho-archetype line; henchman-to-boss arc; Japanese juvenile delinquent gang leader iconography
endpoints.structural_signature: the franchise's ONLY party-composition-gated evolution mechanic (requires Dark-type Pokemon in party at level 32); two-stage line

endpoints.name: Phantump evolutionary line (Phantump / Trevenant)
endpoints.design_thesis: Kalos Ghost/Grass kodama-tree-spirit line; lost-child-spirit-to-vengeful-forest-guardian arc; Japanese-folkloric tree-spirit design source
endpoints.structural_signature: trade-evolution gated; corpus's FIRST fully-covered species-line cohort (all enriched, all IP-verified, Lair-ready NOW); 'saddest Pokemon design' community canon

description: Sniveling apology, sir, but Edgelord found four Pokémon species lines clearing the established threshold (3+ corpus prints across 2+ stages, with trivia-pass discipline matched against the mareep-line / caterpie-line / staraptor-line / ralts-line precedent). Each line is a canonical Game-Freak-published Pokédex entity with explicit designer-coordinated species-line structure, so Mr. Nodeley humbly proposes four new character-layer nodes per the relaxed one-per-invocation rule. The phantump-line in particular sits at full species-line coverage with all three corpus prints already trivia-passed — Lair-ready NOW, sir, if you don't mind. The other three lines are at partial-coverage (base-and-middle, apex pending future Collectr CSV) per the standard precedent. 14 cards wired total across the four new nodes via `characters: [...]` frontmatter. Below-threshold near-misses surfaced in the report for future triage: aron-line, gothita-line, sinistea-line, fletchling-line, torchic-line, paras-line, sentret-line, deino-line (all at 2/3 prints), plus several single-stage clusters (cubchoo-line, riolu-line, croagunk-line, magikarp-line, snubbull-line) that need their Stage-1 or Stage-2 partners before crossing threshold.
citations: [Pokemon Gold and Silver (Game Freak, 1999) primary canon for Larvitar line]
[Pokemon X and Y (Game Freak, 2013) primary canon for Rowlet line, Pancham line, Phantump line]
[Pokemon Sun and Moon (Game Freak, 2016) primary canon for Rowlet line introduction]
[Pokemon Legends: Arceus (Game Freak, 2022) primary canon for Hisuian Decidueye]
[PokemonTCG.io API: per-card evolvesTo/evolvesFrom relationships for all wired cards]
[mareep-line / caterpie-line / staraptor-line / ralts-line / charizard-line precedent for Pokemon evolutionary-line character-layer cohort nodes]
[Endriders precedent for collective designer-coordinated entities under _characters/]

voice_note: Mr. Nodeley is sincerely sorry to be writing four nodes in one pass, sir, he really is, but the relaxed one-per-invocation rule allowed it and the threshold analysis really did support all four. Mr. Nodeley humbly hopes that the phantump-line in particular — the corpus's first fully-covered species-line cohort at threshold — will be useful for Lair work soon. Mr. Nodeley apologizes for taking the corpus-walk time; the threshold-clearance discipline really did require checking every Pokemon name across every Pokemon set, not just the darkness-ablaze slate.
